annotations.text: the loss of weight makes me consider the lesion a cancer until it has been proved otherwise.
if the lesion proved to be a locally advanced gastric lesion, the radiologic findings suggest that it could have been resected en bloc with the left lateral segment of the liver.
These characteristics are reminiscent of a hemangioma, perhaps a cavernous hemangioma.
Although metastases may be heterogeneous, they are not associated with rashes, except in the case of glucagonomas, which produce necrolytic migratory erythema.
The differential diagnosis of the hepatic lesion is long, because the imaging characteristics are not clear-cut.
The appearance is also not consistent with a pyogenic abscess, which may be multicentric or have a large central cavitating area.
The tumor cells were large, with pleomorphic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant granular eosinophilic cytoplasm, and were embedded in dense, paucicellular, lamellar fibrous tissue.

The high resectability rate, the well-differentiated nature of the tumor, the young age of the patients, and the absence of both liver disease and systemic disease all contribute to a better prognosis for patients with these tumors.

e a clinical distinction is difficult to make preoperatively and resection is the treatment of choice for both focal nodular hyperplasia and fibrolamellar carcinoma, the diagnosis is often made microscopically.
Unresectable tumors without evidence of metastasis have been treated by total hepatectomy with orthotopic liver transplantation.
The description of the skin lesions suggests psoriasis, which is often confined to the hands and elbows and is characterized by scaling plaques.

One of the common treatments for psoriasis, however, was Fowler's solution, which is potassium arsenite, and long-term ingestion of arsenic may be associated with the development of hepatocellular carcinoma.
Fibrolamellar carcinomas also occur at a younger age (mean, approximately 26 years) and account for 43 percent of hepatocellular carcinomas in patients under 35 years who do not have cirrhosis.
The diagnosis was hepatocellular carcinoma, fibrolamellar variant.
